diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Inability to complete sentences is a core criterion for severe asthma per national severity classification.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reliever inhaler over-use (6 doses/3h) with diminishing effect

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Refractoriness to repeated bronchodilator indicates severe, treatment-resistant bronchospasm requiring escalated therapy.

symptoms.symptom_name: Visible accessory muscle use and tripod positioning

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective, video-observable marker of significantly increased work of breathing.

symptoms.symptom_name: Poor preventer inhaler adherence over the preceding month

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Under-treated background airway inflammation increases susceptibility to severe exacerbation with a viral trigger.
Hypoxaemia in the context of severe symptoms supports significant airflow obstruction and gas exchange impairment.

symptoms.symptom_name: Widespread polyphonic expiratory wheeze on auscultation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Classic auscultatory finding of bronchospasm; clinician-only finding sealed from telehealth chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reduced peak expiratory flow rate (PEFR) compared to personal best

investigation_details.expected_result: Significantly reduced compared to personal best/predicted, consistent with severe obstruction
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Objective severity grading unavailable via telehealth; should be obtained as part of emergency assessment.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: PEFR <50% predicted/personal best is a defining feature of severe asthma exacerbation.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Incomplete sentences, inhaler refractoriness, accessory muscle use, and SpO2 93% together meet criteria for a severe, potentially life-threatening exacerbation requiring emergency ambulance dispatch.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Peak expiratory flow rate and continuous SpO2 monitoring
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Objective severity grading required as part of emergency assessment; cannot be reliably done via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Nebulised salbutamol and ipratropium, systemic corticosteroids, oxygen
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive emergency treatment for severe exacerbation, deliverable only in person or by paramedics.
Not required routinely for a typical severe asthma exacerbation without suspicion of pneumothorax or consolidation; should not delay bronchodilator therapy.
